015 */
016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc;
017
018import com.fasterxml.jackson.core.JsonProcessingException;
019import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JavaType;
020import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper;
021import org.slf4j.Logger;
022import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
023
024import javax.net.ssl.SSLContext;
025import java.lang.reflect.Type;
026import java.net.URI;
027import java.net.http.HttpClient;
028import java.net.http.HttpRequest;
029import java.net.http.HttpResponse;
030import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
031import java.time.Duration;
032import java.util.Optional;
033import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
034import java.util.concurrent.CompletionException;
035import java.util.function.Function;
036
037/**
038 * Incubating JSON-RPC client using {@link java.net.http.HttpClient}
039 */
040public class JsonRpcClientJavaNet implements JsonRpcTransport<JavaType> {
041    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(JsonRpcClientJavaNet.class);
042
043    private final ObjectMapper mapper;
044    private final URI serverURI;
045    private final String username;
046    private final String password;
047    private final HttpClient client;
048    private static final String UTF8 = StandardCharsets.UTF_8.name();
049
050
051    public JsonRpcClientJavaNet(ObjectMapper mapper, URI server, final String rpcUser, final String rpcPassword) {
052        this(mapper, JsonRpcTransport.getDefaultSSLContext(), server, rpcUser, rpcPassword);
053    }
054
055    public JsonRpcClientJavaNet(ObjectMapper mapper, SSLContext sslContext, URI server, final String rpcUser, final String rpcPassword) {
056        log.debug("Constructing JSON-RPC client for: {}", server);
057        this.mapper = mapper;
058        this.serverURI = server;
059        this.username = rpcUser;
060        this.password = rpcPassword;
061        this.client = HttpClient.newBuilder()
062                .connectTimeout(Duration.ofMinutes(2))
063                .sslContext(sslContext)
064                .build();
065    }
066
067    /**
068     * {@inheritDoc}
069     */
070    @Override
071    public <R> CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<R>> sendRequestForResponseAsync(JsonRpcRequest request, JavaType responseType) {
072        return sendCommon(request)
073                .thenApply(mappingFuncFor(responseType));
074    }
075
076    // For testing only
077    CompletableFuture<String> sendRequestForResponseString(JsonRpcRequest request) {
078        return sendCommon(request);
079    }
080
081    /**
082     * {@inheritDoc}
083     */
084    @Override
085    public URI getServerURI() {
086        return serverURI;
087    }
088
089    private String encodeJsonRpcRequest(JsonRpcRequest request) throws JsonProcessingException {
090        return mapper.writeValueAsString(request);
091    }
092
093    /**
094     * @param request A JSON-RPC request
095     * @return A future for a JSON-RPC response in String format
096     */
097    private CompletableFuture<String> sendCommon(JsonRpcRequest request) {
098        log.debug("Send: {}", request);
099        try {
100            HttpRequest httpRequest = buildJsonRpcPostRequest(request);
101            return client.sendAsync(httpRequest, HttpResponse.BodyHandlers.ofString())
102                    .whenComplete(this::log)
103                    .thenCompose(this::handleStatusError)
104                    .thenApply(HttpResponse::body)
105                    .whenComplete(this::log);
106        } catch (JsonProcessingException e) {
107            return CompletableFuture.failedFuture(e);
108        }
109    }
110
111    /**
112     * Process the HTTP status code. Treats values other than 200 as an error.
113     * @param response A received HTTP response.
114     * @return Completed or failed future as appropriate.
115     */
116    private CompletableFuture<HttpResponse<String>> handleStatusError(HttpResponse<String> response) {
117        if (response.statusCode() != 200) {
118            String body = response.body();
119            log.warn("Bad status code: {}: {}", response.statusCode(), body);
120            log.debug("Headers: {}", response.headers());
121            // Return a failed future containing a JsonRpcStatusException. Create the exception
122            // from a JsonRpcResponse if one can be built, otherwise just use the body string.
123            return CompletableFuture.failedFuture(response.headers()
124                    .firstValue("Content-Type")
125                    .map(s -> s.contains("application/json"))
126                    .flatMap(b -> readErrorResponse(body))
127                    .map(r -> exceptionFrom(response, r))
128                    .orElse(exceptionFrom(response, body))
129            );
130        } else {
131            return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(response);
132        }
133    }
134
135    // Return a JsonRpcException from a non-200 HttpResponse and a successfully read JsonRpcResponse (from the body)
136    // Note that some servers return non-200 status codes when they shouldn't (i.e. when there is an error at the JSON-RPC
137    // protocol level), so we should probably map those cases to return JsonRpcErrorException which is what clients will
138    // want (as it contains useful details about the error.)
139    // TODO: This should probably return a JsonRpcErrorException in most/all cases, since the jsonRpcResponse should
140    // contain more specific and helpful error information.
141    // TODO: Or MAYBE we should even IGNORE the HTTP error in some cases (???) and just return a JsonRpcResponse with the error.
142    // It might be hard to figure out which cases to ignore as it may depend upon the exact protocol and server being used.
143    // Maybe there could be some kind of configuration (perhaps a mapping lambda?) that can be passed to this client
144    // that can be used to map various error combinations of HTTP response codes and JsonRpcResponses to success or error results.
145    private JsonRpcException exceptionFrom(HttpResponse<?> httpResponse, JsonRpcResponse<?> jsonRpcResponse) {
146        return new JsonRpcStatusException(httpResponse.statusCode(), jsonRpcResponse);
147    }
148
149    // Return a JsonRpcException from a non-200 HttpResponse and a body that can't be parsed to a JsonRpcResponse.
150    private JsonRpcException exceptionFrom(HttpResponse<?> httpResponse, String body) {
151        return new JsonRpcStatusException(httpResponse.statusCode(), body);
152    }
153
154    // Try to read a JsonRpcResponse from a string (error case)
155    private Optional<JsonRpcResponse<Object>> readErrorResponse(String body) {
156        JsonRpcResponse<Object> response;
157        try {
158            response = mapper.readValue(body, responseTypeFor(Object.class));
159        } catch (JsonProcessingException e) {
160            response = null;
161        }
162        return Optional.ofNullable(response);
163    }
164
165    private HttpRequest buildJsonRpcPostRequest(JsonRpcRequest request) throws JsonProcessingException {
166        String requestString = encodeJsonRpcRequest(request);
167        return buildJsonRpcPostRequest(requestString);
168    }
169
170    private HttpRequest buildJsonRpcPostRequest(String requestString) {
171        log.info("request is: {}", requestString);
172
173        String auth = username + ":" + password;
174        String basicAuth = "Basic " + JsonRpcTransport.base64Encode(auth);
175
176        return HttpRequest
177                .newBuilder(serverURI)
178                .header("Content-Type", "application/json;charset=" +  UTF8)
179                .header("Accept-Charset", UTF8)
180                .header("Accept", "application/json")
181                .header("Authorization", basicAuth)
182                .POST(HttpRequest.BodyPublishers.ofString(requestString))
183                .build();
184    }
185
186    // return a MappingFunction for a given type
187    private <R, T extends Type> MappingFunction<R> mappingFuncFor(T responseType) {
188        return s -> mapper.readValue(s, (JavaType) responseType);
189    }
190
191    /**
192     * Map a response string to a Java object. Wraps checked {@link JsonProcessingException}
193     * in unchecked {@link CompletionException}.
194     * @param <R> result type
195     */
196    @FunctionalInterface
197    protected interface MappingFunction<R> extends Function<String, R> {
198
199        /**
200         * Gets a result. Wraps checked {@link JsonProcessingException} in {@link CompletionException}
201         * @param s input
202         * @return a result
203         * @throws CompletionException (unchecked) if a JsonProcessingException exception occurs
204         */
205        @Override
206        default R apply(String s) throws CompletionException {
207            try {
208                return applyThrows(s);
209            } catch (Exception e) {
210                throw new CompletionException(e);
211            }
212        }
213
214        /**
215         * Gets a result and may throw a checked exception.
216         * @param s input
217         * @return a result
218         * @throws JsonProcessingException Checked Exception
219         */
220        R applyThrows(String s) throws Exception;
221    }
222
223    /**
224     * Logging action for a {@code CompletionStage} that returns {@link HttpResponse}
225     * <p>
226     * Note that an error at this layer should be treated as a warning for logging purposes, because network
227     * errors are relatively common and should be handled and/or logged at higher layers of the stack.
228     * @param httpResponse non-null on success
229     * @param t non-null on error
230     */
231    private void log(HttpResponse<String> httpResponse, Throwable t) {
232        if ((httpResponse != null)) {
233            log.info("log data string: {}", httpResponse);
234        } else {
235            log.warn("exception: ", t);
236        }
237    }
238
239    /**
240     * Logging action for a {@code CompletionStage} that returns {@link String}. In the current
241     * implementation of this client, that should be a JSON-formatted string.
242     * <p>
243     * Note that an error at this layer should be treated as a warning for logging purposes, because network
244     * errors are relatively common and should be handled and/or logged at higher layers of the stack.
245     * @param s non-null on success
246     * @param t non-null on error
247     */
248    private void log(String s, Throwable t) {
249        if (s != null) {
250            log.info("log data string: {}", s.substring(0 ,Math.min(100, s.length())));
251        } else {
252            log.warn("exception: ", t);
253        }
254    }
255
256    private JavaType responseTypeFor(Class<?> resultType) {
257        return mapper.getTypeFactory().
258                constructParametricType(JsonRpcResponse.class, resultType);
259    }
260}
